hq: 100-7405 Rte Transcanadienne, Montreal QC H4T1Z2, Canada

system_description.overview: Lilee is an AI-powered platform that automates and streamlines business workflows. The system integrates with EHR systems (via AcuityNXT), uses AI agents and workflows (via Mastra Cloud), and provides document and data automation services. Infrastructure is hosted on Supabase (carve-out subservice organization) with a Next.js 14 frontend on Vercel.

products.Lilee: AI-powered workflow and document automation platform integrating with EHR authorization APIs and AI agents/workflows

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Supabase — carve-out method (subservice organization with its own SOC 2 report)
regions: Not specifically named; multi-AZ described via Supabase
infrastructure.physical_presence: 100-7405 Rte Transcanadienne, Montreal QC H4T1Z2 (registered address); no physical servers
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (high availability via Supabase multi-AZ failover)
additional_cloud: Mastra Cloud (AI Workflows, Agents & Async Jobs/Queues)
serverless: Vercel Edge (Next.js 14 API routes / Step Functions API Routes)
infrastructure.cdn: Vercel (Edge network for static assets and frontend)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in Supabase VPC with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Customer accounts are appropriately segmented from each other.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Vercel Next.js 14 API routes (apps/web/src/app/api) acting as API layer
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services; reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Supabase to identify and protect against threats
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; email scanning to prevent malicious scripts
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> Clerk (Auth-as-a-Service, OIDC/OAuth) -> Vercel (Next.js 14 on Edge) -> Step Functions API Routes (apps/web/src/app/api) -> AcuityNXT (EHR Authorization API via HTTP) + Mastra Cloud (AI Workflows, Mastra Agents & Workflows, Async Jobs/Queues) + Supabase (Postgres DB, Auth & RLS JWT, Object Storage, Audit Logs/Monitoring)

application_architecture.pattern: Serverless-heavy (Vercel Edge + Supabase managed services + Mastra Cloud AI workers)
application_architecture.api_style: REST (Step Functions API routes via Next.js)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development and production environments; changes tested before migration to production

databases: Supabase Postgres (managed, with Row-Level Security via JWT)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Not specified (backup policy exists; backup and restoration logs maintained for defined retention periods)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Clerk (Auth-as-a-Service, OIDC/OAuth) for user-facing auth; Supabase Auth with JWT/RLS for data access; Supabase password policy for server/database accounts
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all administrative access to production platform; MFA required to access source code management tool; 2FA for employee email
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Supabase password policy with complexity requirements; periodic forced changes; account lockout after failed attempts; no guest/anonymous logins

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (Supabase KMS) used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: Supabase KMS; access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA access; branch rules configured)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to move change to production environment (CC8.1.2 confirmed)
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback (baseline configurations retained as code in infrastructure)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tools for capacity/anomaly detection)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Supabase Audit Logs (implied); logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configuration changes
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high and critical risk events; resolved via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary infrastructure subservice — Postgres DB, Auth & RLS (JWT), Object Storage, Audit Logs/Monitoring, VPC, KMS
Frontend hosting and CDN — Next.js 14 on Edge, serverless API routes
EHR Authorization API integration via HTTP

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; definitions updated weekly); also on all desktops/laptops (definitions updated daily)
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications (conducted by internal IT team); self-assessment scan prior to every production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center; Supabase is responsible for physical security (carve-out)
A1.2 — Environmental protections and software/data backup recovery infrastructure; managed by Supabase/building management
Processing Integrity — determined not relevant to the system by Lilee management
Privacy — determined not relevant to the system by Lilee management
notable_controls: CC8.1.2: Branch protection rules require additional approval for all merge requests to production
CC8.1.3: Source code access restricted to authorized users with MFA
CC8.1.4: Separate development and production environments; changes tested before migration
CC6.1.1: MFA enforced for all administrative access to production platform
CC6.2.2: Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged
CC6.1.4: Access revoked within one business day of termination
CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC7.1.2: Monthly automated vulnerability scanning by internal IT team
CC7.4.1: BCDR policy tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Supabase SOC 2 report reviewed annually as part of vendor management (CC9.1)

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-AZ via Supabase, CDN via Vercel Edge, IaC (config-as-code). Entirely delegated to Supabase as subservice org — no direct cloud provider control. No named region, no multi-region, single subservice provider. Vercel adds CDN/edge benefit but also adds another critical dependency.

application_architecture.rationale: Modern serverless architecture: Next.js 14 on Vercel Edge, Supabase managed services, Mastra Cloud for AI workflows, Clerk for auth. Multiple specialized services rather than monolith. No languages/frameworks beyond Next.js disclosed. No container orchestration. Architecture is relatively modern but small team (4 engineers).

data_layer.rationale: Supabase Postgres with Row-Level Security (JWT), Supabase Object Storage. No caching layer disclosed. No data warehouse. Backup policy exists but frequency not specified. Per-tenant segregation via Supabase RLS. Multi-AZ via Supabase.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen testing, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS via Supabase, firewalls, MFA for admin access and source code, antivirus on servers (weekly) and endpoints (daily definitions), quarterly access reviews, 1-day revocation SLA, KMS encryption, content filtering. No WAF named, no SIEM named, no cybersecurity insurance.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, separate environments, emergency change process, config-as-code (unnamed tool), automatic patch management. CI/CD tool not named, IaC tool not named, source control tool not named. Vercel handles deployment pipeline implicitly.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, reviewed and tested annually across multiple loss scenarios. Multi-AZ failover via Supabase. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, backup frequency not specified. BCDR heavily dependent on Supabase's own DR capabilities.

vendor_diversity.rationale: 5 critical vendors identified (Supabase, Vercel, Clerk, Mastra Cloud, AcuityNXT). Good functional separation with specialized vendors for each layer. However, Supabase handles DB + Auth + Storage + Monitoring — high concentration in a single subservice org. AcuityNXT is a critical external dependency for EHR integration.

overall.rationale: Modern serverless stack with good security hygiene for a small team. Solid vendor ecosystem (Supabase + Vercel + Clerk + Mastra) with zero exceptions across 29 controls. Key weaknesses: entire infrastructure delegated to Supabase as subservice org, no RTO/RPO, backup frequency unspecified, no SIEM or WAF named, very small team (4 engineers). SOC 2 Type 1 only — no operational testing period.

red_flags: Entire infrastructure delegated to Supabase as subservice org (carve-out) — Lilee has no direct cloud control; Supabase outage = Lilee outage
No RTO or RPO disclosed — BCDR maturity cannot be quantified
No WAF named — web application attack surface unclear
No SIEM tool named — security event correlation capability unknown
CI/CD tool, IaC tool, and source control tool names not disclosed — tooling quality unverifiable
SOC 2 Type 1 only — no operational testing period; controls not tested over time
yellow_flags: Very small engineering team (4 people: 1 Founding Engineer, 1 Software Engineer, 1 Intern) — single points of failure risk
No backup frequency specified — data recovery window unknown
No caching layer disclosed — potential performance concern at scale
No multi-region failover — regional Supabase outage would impact service
AcuityNXT EHR dependency — third-party API availability affects core functionality
Metadata listed infra_provider as 'Render' — actual infrastructure is Supabase (discrepancy in company-provided data)
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
MFA enforced for all administrative access, source code management, and employee email (2FA)
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Access revocation within one business day of termination
BCDR plan tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Branch protection with mandatory approval for all production merges (CC8.1.2)
Separate development and production environments with testing gate (CC8.1.4)
Antivirus on production servers (weekly definitions) and all desktops/laptops (daily definitions)
Content filtering via proxy server with email scanning
Supabase SOC 2 report reviewed annually (vendor management)
Zero exceptions across all 29 tested controls
Clerk (dedicated auth-as-a-service) for OIDC/OAuth — modern auth architecture
Supabase RLS (Row-Level Security via JWT) for per-tenant data segregation
key_observations: Lilee's infrastructure metadata listed 'Render' as the infra provider — the actual PDF reveals Supabase as the primary subservice organization with no mention of Render anywhere; this discrepancy warrants clarification

The architecture is notably modern for a small startup: Next.js 14 on Vercel Edge + Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage) + Clerk (auth) + Mastra Cloud (AI agents) — each layer delegated to a specialized managed service rather than self-operated
Mastra Cloud for AI workflows/agents is an unusual and noteworthy architectural choice — indicates the company is building AI-native features with dedicated async job infrastructure
AcuityNXT EHR Authorization API integration suggests healthcare or medical adjacent use case — adds regulatory consideration beyond SOC 2

Trust criteria scope is Security + Availability + Confidentiality; Processing Integrity and Privacy were explicitly declared NOT relevant to the system by management — this is a meaningful scope decision for an AI platform handling business documents
The team has 4 engineers total (Founding Engineer + Software Engineer + Intern + CEO who appears technical) — extremely lean for a company pursuing SOC 2 certification with 3 trust criteria
HQ is Montreal, QC — Canadian company subject to PIPEDA/Quebec Law 25 in addition to SOC 2; Privacy trust criteria exclusion is notable given Quebec's stricter privacy law
Governance structure: Board has 3 members (Olivier Trudeau CEO, Antonino Piazza, Vincent Richard) — suggests institutional backing despite small team size
